respectueuse de l’expérience utilisateur.RUN - Amélioration continue & structuration de l’équipe Compliance (25 %) :Participer à l’optimisation des process KYC et LCB-FT : analyse des points de friction, automatisation possible, coordination avec les autres équipes.Contribuer à la veille réglementaire opérationnelle : suivre les évolutions et aider à leur intégration dans les pratiques internes.Identifier et proposer des améliorations pour renforcer l’efficacité, la traçabilité ou la qualité de nos contrôles.Maintenir à jour la documentation interne (guides, procédures, FAQ internes, matrices de contrôle, etc.).Participer aux recettes fonctionnelles des outils liés à la compliance (app client, back office, outil de transaction monitoring…).Tu deviens le relai terrain de la compliance pour faire évoluer nos outils et méthodes.BUILD - Projets ad hoc & missions transverses (10%) :Contribuer à des projets stratégiques : mise en place d’un outil de monitoring intelligent, revue de notre politique LCB-FT, indicateurs de pilotage de l’activité, etc.Proposer des initiatives selon tes appétences : automatisation, data, sensibilisation interne, documentation, etc.Participer aux projets transverses menés avec les autres équipes en interne.Tu as l’espace pour monter en compétence et développer ta propre expertise.🦊 Bref, si tu veux apprendre vite, avoir de l’impact, et travailler sur des sujets de fond dans une équipe où la rigueur rime avec bienveillance, on t’attend.Vos compétencesCe que tu as pour Green-GotTu es jeune diplômé (formation supérieure Bac+2 à Bac+5) en finance, audit, gestion des risques, contrôle, droit, ou toute formation complémentaire à ces domaines,Tu n’es peut-être pas encore expert en conformité, mais tu es curieux, rigoureux, autonome, et tu as une vraie envie d’apprendre et de te former à un métier porteur de sens ;Tu fais preuve d’un excellent esprit d’analyse, tu aimes comprendre les mécanismes complexes et trouver des solutions concrètes ;Tu as une vraie capacité d'organisation, tu sais prioriser, structurer et suivre des dossiers avec sérieux ;Tu n’as pas peur des acronymes (KYC, LCB-FT, AML, RGPD...), au contraire, ils t’intriguent ;Tu es à l’aise à l’écrit comme à l’oral, y compris avec des personnes externes (clients ou partenaires) ;Tu sais faire preuve de diplomatie et de pédagogie, même quand tu dois dire non ou demander un justificatif délicat ;Tu es à l’aise avec les outils numériques et les environnements dynamiques (Slack, Notion, CRM, outils KYC, etc.) ;Tu es motivé par notre mission : mettre la finance au service de la transition écologique et sociale ;Bonus : tu as une bonne maîtrise de l’anglais (ou tu es prêt à la renforcer) – certains de nos partenaires et documents sont en anglais ;Encore plus bonus, mais vraiment pas obligatoire : tu as une passion pour les règlements européens, les systèmes d’information et les bases de données bien rangées. (On a le droit.)AvantagesCe que Green-Got a pour toi😍 Équipe : Un environnement de travail international, avec une équipe ambitieuse, jeune, déterminée qui passe de bons moments ensemble !🌎 Impact : L’opportunité de contribuer à un projet qui aura un réel impact positif sur notre environnement.🤝 Confiance : Pouvoir prendre rapidement des responsabilités au sein de l’équipe.🚀 Perspectives : En tant que leader sur le marché des fintechs vertes, notre croissance est rapide — les profils les plus engagés auront l’opportunité de grandir avec nous et de prendre des responsabilités clés !🙌 Cadre de travail : Le poste est idéalement basé à Paris (2-3 jours par semaine au bureau), mais nous restons ouverts à d’excellents profils en full remote si besoin.Au fait, moi c’est Justé, Compliance Manager.Process de recrutement:Tu nous envoies un CV et tout autre élément qui pourrait nous aider à jauger tes compétences et ta motivation.S’il est sélectionné, je te passerai un rapide coup de fil (15min) pour discuter du poste, puis je t’enverrai un Business Case (environ 1-2h) à préparer et la restitution se fera lors d’un entretien d’environ 1h.Tu passeras ensuite 3 autres entretiens :  Un avec un membre de l’équipe,Un avec Adèle, la Head of Operations,Un avec Andréa, le CEO.
title: Compliance Analyst - CDI

companyDescription:
At American Express, we know that with the right backing, people and businesses have the power to progress in incredible ways. Whether we’re supporting our customers’ financial confidence to move ahead, taking commerce to new heights, or encouraging people to explore the world, our colleagues are constantly striving to uphold our powerful backing promise to our customers and each other every day. 
 
These beliefs have been our North Star for 170 years as our business transformed – from helping evacuate travelers during World Wars, to ensuring the safety of our customers’ funds during the Great Depression in the U.S., to creating the Shop Small® movement to help small businesses recover from the Financial Crisis, to providing aid to communities impacted by many natural disasters and so much more.  
 
For generations, the key to our success has been the determination and resilience of our American Express colleagues. Now, as a globally integrated payments company, we work together to provide customers with access to products, insights and world-class experiences that enrich lives and build business success. Join us and let’s lead the way together. 

Learn more about us at: 
https://www.americanexpress.com/careers
https://www.americanexpress.com/
https://www.facebook.com/AmericanExpressUS
https://www.instagram.com/americanexpress/
https://twitter.com/americanexpress

See our community guidelines at:

descriptionHtml:
La culture d’American Express repose sur 175 ans de tradition d’innovation, de valeurs et de comportements de leadership partagés,et un engagement inébranlable à soutenir nos clients, communautés et collègues. En tant que membre de l’équipe Amex, vous bénéficierez de ce puissant soutien avec une prise en charge complète de votre bien-être et de nombreuses opportunités d’acquérir de nouvelles compétences, de développer votre leadership et de faire évoluer votre carrière.

Ici, votre avis et vos idées comptent, votre travail a un impact et ensemble, vous nous aiderez à définir l’avenir d’American Express.


Quel sera votre impact sur ce poste?  

Missions principales :
  • Participer à la veille réglementaire et superviser le la mise en œuvre des exigences légales et réglementaires, ainsi que les procédures et politiques internes pour la nouvelle entité.
  • Fournir des recommandations et conseils aux différents départements sur les sujets relatifs à la conformité, relatifs aux obligations associées à l’acquisition et à la gestion des commerçants à Monaco et à Saint Barthélémy.
  • Participer à la production et la soumission des reporting règlementaires à destination des autorités de compétence à Monaco et à Saint Barthélémy, ainsi que les reporting internes au Senior Management.
  • D’autres missions définies pourront être ajoutés à ce poste en fonction des évolutions de celui-ci. 
Missions complémentaires:
  • Contribuer au déploiement du programme de conformité
  • Participer à l’analyse et à la gestion des risques réglementaires
  • Identifier les risques de non-conformité, superviser les processus de remédiation, et mettre à jour les procédures impactées
  • Participer à des projets d'amélioration de l'organisation de la conformité
  • Participation aux rapports annuels à destination des autorités de tutelle ; d’information des membres de la direction/comites de gouvernance et d’autres rapports internes nécessaires au bon fonctionnement du programme
  • Travailler en étroite collaboration avec les autres lignes de défense
  • Piloter les travaux de cartographie des risques de non-conformité, les dispositifs de maîtrise des risques et les plans d’action à mettre en place
  • Accompagner et préparer les audits internes ainsi que les examens réglementaires

 

Profil et compétences recherchés : 
  • Maîtrise de la réglementation européenne notamment en matière de services de paiement, protection des données personnelles, lutte anti-blanchiment et financement du terrorisme, etc.
  • Une connaissance approfondie des aspects réglementaires et légaux applicables au système bancaire/financier à Monaco et à Saint Barthélémy.
  • Diplôme requis : Master 2 en Droit ou en conformité ou expérience équivalente.
  • Minimum trois ans d’expérience professionnelle sur un poste similaire 
  • Expérience en gestion d’équipe
  • Anglais courant à l’écrit et à l’oral
  • Sens de la rigueur et de la précision ainsi qu’une réelle capacité d’adaptation vous permettant de gérer simultanément de multiples priorités.
  • Prise d’initiative, pro activité dans la résolution de problèmes
  • Bon relationnel, capacité à travailler dans une organisation avec des interlocuteurs variés et à évoluer dans un environnement international.
  • Sens de l’humour.
Non-considerations for sponsorship: Employment eligibility to work with American Express in the [Enter Country Here] is required as the company will not pursue visa sponsorship for these positions. Considerations for sponsorship: Depending on factors such as business unit requirements, the nature of the position, cost and applicable laws, American Express may provide visa sponsorship for certain positions. 
Nous soutenons nos collaborateurs avec des avantages et des programmes qui favorisent leur bien-être global, leur santé physique, financière et mentale, à chaque étape de la vie :
  • Salaires de base et variables compétitifs avec la possibilité d’être associé à la réussite de la société. 
  • Mutuelle d’entreprise et assurance-vie. 
  • Programme de bien-être de l’entreprise axé sur la promotion du bien-être physique et mental. 
  • Travailler sur site, selon un modèle hybride.
  • Programme de bien-être de l’entreprise axé sur la promotion du bien-être physique et mental. 
  • Des expériences d’apprentissage innovantes qui vous permettent de développer de nouvelles compétences, d’atteindre vos objectifs de carrière et de vous développer en tant que leader à tous les niveaux. 
  • Aide à la garde d’enfants.

Chez American Express, les offres d’emploi sont conditionnées à la réussite de la vérification des antécédents, sous réserve des lois et réglementations applicables.

descriptionHtml:
Job Description:

Job Title: Compliance and Operational Risk Manager - Global Corporate and Investment Banking

Corporate Title: Director

Location: Paris

Company Overview:

At Bank of America, we are guided by a common purpose to help make financial lives better through the power of every connection. We do this by driving Responsible Growth and delivering for our clients, teammates, communities and shareholders every day.

Being a Great Place to Work is core to how we drive Responsible Growth. This includes our commitment to being a diverse and inclusive workplace, attracting and developing exceptional talent, supporting our teammates’ physical, emotional, and financial wellness, recognizing and rewarding performance, and how we make an impact in the communities we serve.

At Bank of America, you can build a successful career with opportunities to learn, grow, and make an impact. Join us!

Location Overview:

Based in the 8th arrondissement and within the Parisian Golden Triangle, our beautiful Art-Deco era office benefits from luxury modern workspaces, an abundance of natural light and is easily accessible by public transport.

Role Overview:

An opportunity has arisen in our Paris office for a forward-thinking professional to lead our second line of defence Compliance and Operational Risk coverage of Global Corporate and Investment Banking for Bank of America Europe DAC (‘BofA Europe’). You will execute on the firm’s Global Compliance and Operational Risk (‘GCOR’) programme specialising in the day-to-day Compliance coverage of Private Side businesses.

This role is responsible for executing second line of defence compliance and operational risk oversight for the Front Line Unit. Key responsibilities include ensuring requirements of the Global Compliance Enterprise Policy, the Operational Risk Management Enterprise Policy, the Compliance and Operational Risk Management Program and Standard Operating Procedures are implemented appropriately, and identifying, challenging, escalating, and mitigating risks in a timely manner.

Responsibilities:

  • Provide ongoing Compliance and Operational Risk coverage for the Private Side businesses in BofA Europe with principal focus being the Global Corporate & Investment Banking (‘GCIB’) business in the entity and more broadly across EMEA. Provide additional support to other EMEA GCIB coverage areas as required.
  • Provide dedicated, timely guidance on the relevant Laws, Rules and Regulations (‘LRR’) applicable to the entity and its businesses including risk and impact assessments on new product initiatives, specific transactions, ad hoc queries, routine risk assessment processes such as the RCSA etc.
  • Be proficient in assessing risks, associated controls and their effectiveness, while driving compliance with applicable LRRs and regulatory guidelines, adhering to policies, and developing reporting and documentation.
  • Engage in activities to provide support to the GCOR function to provide independent 2nd line oversight of Front Line Unit ownership and management of Compliance and Operational risks in alignment with the Compliance and Operational Risk Management Program and Standard Operating Procedures.
  • Monitor the regulatory environment to identify regulatory changes applicable to area(s) of coverage, and execute Targeted Assessments aligned to relevant themes or trends.
  • Ensure that Monitoring & Testing is conducted in line with GCOR programme requirements and on a timely basis with appropriate escalation and resolution of any issues or matters arising.
  • Assist in identifying and escalating control deficiencies or issues that arise and drive actions to address the root causes that lead to compliance or operational risk issues and/or losses.
  • Assist in the development of independent risk management reporting for respective area(s) of coverage as input into management routines.
  • As a member of the BofA Europe – Paris Branch Local Management Team (LMT) be accountable for reporting on and escalating Paris Branch Compliance & Op Risk matters
  • Build effective working relationships with colleagues including Regional LoB Compliance & Operational Risk, EU Country Compliance & Operational Risk, GFCC, the Control Room, other Lines of Business, and across regions
  • Assist in responding to regulatory inquiries as well as other audits and examinations.
  • Review and challenge relevant processes and risks, including those defined in Single Process Inventory (SPI).

What we are looking for:

  • A versatile, confident individual with significant, relevant work experience in a similar GCIB compliance and/or operational risk role, with a detailed understanding of institutional private side business and banking products
  • Confident written and verbal communication skills
  • Ability to engage and communicate confidently and professionally with the business, as well as other internal and external stakeholders
  • Knowledge of regulatory frameworks relevant to an EU authorised Credit Institution.
  • Ability to interpret relevant laws, rules, regulations and guidelines and draft/implement related policies and procedures.

Benefits of working at Bank of America:

France

  • Competitive retirement plan in addition to State plans
  • Mandatory Medical Plan provided by the bank as a top-up to Social Security health benefits for you and your family.
  • Employees and family members can receive free health advice 24/7 through a range of English and French speaking medical providers
  • Life and disability insurance
  • Time Savings Account (‘CET’) to save some of your leave days, cash them out or use them later
  • Reimbursement of 50% of your commuter pass
  • Nursery scheme for children under age 3
  • Works council benefits
  • Access to an Employee Assistance Programme for confidential support and help for everyday matters
  • Opportunity to give back to your community, develop new skills and work with new groups of people by volunteering with local charities
  • Opportunity to receive free entry to arts exhibitions sponsored by Bank of America in Paris

Bank of America:

Good conduct and sound judgment is crucial to our long term success. It’s important that all employees in the organisation understand the expected standards of conduct and how we manage conduct risk. Individual accountability and an ownership mind-set are the cornerstones of our Code of Conduct and are at the heart of managing risk well.

We are an equal opportunities employer and ensure that no applicant is subject to less favourable treatment on the grounds of gender/sex, gender identity or gender reassignment, marital or civil partner status, race, religion or belief, colour, nationality, ethnic or national origins, membership of the Traveller community, age, sexual orientation, pregnancy or maternity, civil status, socio-economic background, responsibility for dependants, family status or physical or mental disability. The Bank selects candidates for interview based on their skills, qualifications and experience.

We strive to ensure that our recruitment processes are accessible for all candidates and encourage any candidates to tell us about any adjustment requirements.

companyDescription: As an integral part of AXA, we create innovative technology and data solutions to help AXA fulfill its ambition of being a customer-focused, tech-led company. Axa Group Operations is a young and dynamic division launched in 2019 and comprises 6,700 employees across 14 countries all around the globe from Paris to Pune. We are committed to being an inclusive employer. We recruit colleagues who reflect the diversity of cultures and perspectives in our various locations across several continents.
companySlogan: Striving to Unlock the Future through Technology, Data, and Operations, as part of the AXA group.
descriptionHtml:
About AXA

As a world-leading insurance company, we act for human progress by protecting what matters. With 153,000 employees in 54 countries working for 105 million customers, we’ve created a truly dynamic and vibrant community. Inclusion and diversity link closely with our values, and together we’re nurturing a culture of respect, for each other, for our customers and the communities around us. Join AXA and you’ll feel like you belong, are included and can thrive. You’ll be able to shape the way you work and truly grow your potential as you seek out new opportunities, push boundaries and benefit people in critical moments of their lives. This is your chance to build the tomorrow you want. Know you can.

About The Entity

AXA is becoming a sustainable tech-led company and at AXA Group Operations we are one of the major catalysts for this transformation.

We set the tone by triggering and empowering the evolution of our insurance business model through technology and innovation, driving its concrete implementation globally at speed, with a high quality of advisory and execution.

We are present across 17 countries with committed, highly qualified teams. We leverage technology, data, sourcing, security and investment allocation in a global way, but also achieve economies of scale and synergies when necessary.

At AXA Group Operations, we want to be recognized in three fields of action:

  • State-of-the-art Data Technology to drive customer experience
  • State-of-the-art Procurement & Sourcing to drive efficiency and better manage risks
  • High-Performing Global Team for stronger partnerships with AXA entities

About The Job

The Compliance Assurance & Project Manager is responsible for ensuring that the organization adheres to all relevant laws, regulations, and internal policies. This role involves conducting compliance audits, developing risk management strategies, and providing training to staff on compliance matters. The Compliance Assurance & Project Manager reports to the Compliance Assurance Manager which is managing a team based in Paris and Spain, supporting AXA GO on Compliance matters from financial crime to geopolitical compliance needs, local third-party compliance, Intragroup Contracts and International regulatory matters. The activities cover both the prevention and the remediation, and act as a strategic Business Partner vis-à-vis AXA GO team as well as the other AXA operational entities fronted.

As Compliance Assurance & Project Manager, your main objective and purpose is to:

  • Protect AXA GO and its top management against sanctions or fines by addressing compliance and data privacy risks identification against both international and local regulations. Implement adequate assessment of the impacts across AXA GO’s divisions, monitoring, and remediation design / implementation.
  • Raise attention on compliance risks and control and help decision making by incorporating innovative problem-solving skills.

Main missions

Responsibilities

The Compliance Assurance & Project Manager will personally be involved in cases covering matters in compliance assurance and will notably be responsible for:

Compliance Control Framework

  • Contribute to the various compliance, data privacy and AI risk assessments across AXA GO and its subsidiaries based in Europe, Asia and Americas. Ensure adequate reporting to the Senior Management and to the AXA Group Compliance and Data Privacy functions, follow up and control along the year any relevant variation on the risk. Guarantee escalation to Senior Management on a timely manner when deemed necessary.
  • Contribute to the Compliance Assurance plan with the aim to control the alignment of the Compliance activities (implementation requirements, policies and procedures, trainings and awareness, etc.) with AXA Group Standards and external regulations.
  • Ensure strong alignment in terms of practices, control frameworks and control review with other GO control functions (e.g. Risk Management, Internal Control, Internal Audit) but also other support functions where there is a strong need for coordination on recurrent activities such as the Third-Party Risk Management process (Vendor Risk Committee, Third Party Audits) with Procurement teams or the ISO certification process with Information Security team.
  • Provide both guidance and regulatory direction to develop new processes or controls. Foster transformation, innovation in the compliance landscape, implementation of best practices, identify areas of improvement and simplification.

Regulatory transformation

  • Lead projects with the objective to accompany the Compliance & DP department in its transformation journey with regards to the need to comply with new regulations but also to improve technical capabilities (e.g. more automation, AI use cases, etc.).
  • Report and escalate any relevant change in regulations subject to generate additional risks or needs inside AXA GO’s environment.
  • Drive the change by leading transformation projects for the benefit of the Compliance & Data Privacy team and the other AXA GO teams.

Global governance

  • Collaborate closely with the other teams reporting to the Compliance & Data Privacy department.
  • Coordinate transversal initiatives/projects and work cross-functionally with the different teams reporting to the General Secretary & Risk Division (Risk Management, Internal Control, Legal, etc.) as well as other GO Divisions (HR, Finance) and Group Corporate Functions hosted within AXA GO (IT, Procurement, etc.). Those initiatives would benefit to AXA GO but also to other AXA operating entities that AXA GO is serving.
  • Pilot and coordinate strategic Compliance & DP deliverables to be reported to GO Senior Management (Vendor Risk Committee, Risk & Compliance Committee, Executive Committee, Board of Directors) and/or to AXA Group functions and/or strategic committees. Ensure consistency between the different deliverables.
  • Prepare, collect, analyze and synthesize relevant inputs/pre-reading information for the Head of Compliance & Data Privacy Officer prior operational meetings or strategic committees.

Monitoring awareness

  • Maintain an updated knowledge of AXA GO teams and Senior Management about local and global regulatory updates and trends.
  • Contribute to the overall monitoring of the Compliance & Data Privacy training plan and ensure that all employees and teams exposed the specific compliance risks (HR, Proc., Executive Members, etc.) are sufficiently trained in alignment with Group standards and external regulations requirements.
  • With the support of the other teams reporting to the Compliance and Data Privacy teams, continuously monitor changes in regulations and industry standards. Evaluate how these changes affect AXA GO and its operations and inform relevant stakeholders about their implications. Conduct training session and control a posteriori to identify potential areas of non-compliance and implement corrective actions.

Expected Skills & Experience

We are looking for someone with the following experience and skills:

Experience

  • 7+ years' solid experience in Compliance, Risk Management and/or Internal Control in technology, insurance and/ or regulated sectors both in-house or from consultancy firm(s), preferably international entities
  • Operational knowledge on one or several of the following areas: General compliance, Financial Crime, International Sanctions, Risk Management, Data Privacy or Internal Control
  • Language skills: full professional proficiency in English
  • Notions of the insurance or health sectors are a plus

Technical Skills

  • Demonstrated success working in a high-performing, business results-driven environment
  • Critical thinking skills and experience assessing risks, measuring impacts, and building mitigation solutions with partners
  • Persuade and influence using compelling arguments to gain the support and commitment of others
  • Strong European compliance and technological regulatory knowledge.
  • Ability to design and direct operational implementation across AXA GO’s departments
  • Fast build a thorough knowledge of the company and its processes
  • Strong compliance / risk analytical skills, business mindset with a risk orientation. Use of the appropriate criteria to regularly evaluate company processes and compliance risk, challenge of the status-quo and ineffective processes and promote constructive alternatives
  • Ability to effectively communicate with and convince management, win the trust the different actors in the organization while challenging and fostering change
  • Ability to build strong working relationships across a broad and varied mix of people and skills in IT and across business organizations

Soft Skills / Transversal Skills

  • Excellent communication skills at all levels, including the capacity to explain concisely complex legal topics in simple operational ways to non-compliance collaborators
  • Language skills: full professional proficiency in English
  • Strong work ethic, comfortable working in a fast-paced environment with cross-functional teams, international and collegial corporate culture
  • Self-motivated, creative, and autonomous
  • Positive mindset, collaborative work style and team-building orientation

What We Offer

We bring together the expertise, cultural diversity and creativity of over 8,000 employees worldwide and we’re committed to equal opportunities in all aspects of employment (gender, LGBT+, disabled persons, or people of different origins) and to promoting Diversity & Inclusion by creating a work environment where all employees are treated with dignity and respect, and where individual differences are valued.

With 153,000 employees in 54 countries working for 105 million customers, we’ve created a truly dynamic and vibrant community. Inclusion and diversity link closely with our values, and together we’re nurturing a culture of respect, for each other, for our customers and the communities around us. Join AXA and you’ll feel like you belong, are included and can thrive. You’ll be able to shape the way you work and truly grow your potential as you seek out new opportunities, push boundaries and benefit people in critical moments of their lives. This is your chance to build the tomorrow you want. companyDescription: Founded in 2001, Oaklen Consulting is an independent consulting and auditing firm specialising in payments. We have developed a high level of expertise that is recognised by our clients, whom we support in their payment transformation projects from strategic vision to implementation. This global approach to payment methods allows us to intervene in the entire value chain. The success of the ambitious projects entrusted to us by our clients is based on our sense of commitment and the expertise of our consultants involved.
companySlogan: Matching the Worlds
descriptionHtml:
Votre mission essentielle : garantir une économie en mouvement, en accompagnant tous les secteurs afin de façonner au mieux les moyens de paiement les plus efficaces.

Au sein de nos équipes multidisciplinaires, et grâce à notre Académie des Paiements interne, vous serez amené·e à conduire des missions auprès de nos clients Corporate sur des verticales très variées (Luxe & Retail, Santé, T&E, Médias, Energie, …) sur l'ensemble des problématiques du paiement, allant des problématiques de Pay In jusqu’au Pay Out (marketing de l'offre, mise en marché, innovation, technologie et plateformisation, règlementation et conformité, cybersécurité).

Au cours de ces missions, vous développerez les compétences fondamentales du métier de consultant·e (capacité d’analyse et de synthèse, organisation, communication, créativité et qualité de production) et gagnerez rapidement une connaissance approfondie du secteur du paiement, tant par l'intermédiaire les missions que grâce à l’esprit d’équipe d'Oaklen Consulting et aux liens forts que vous entretiendrez avec vos managers et collaborateurs·trices.

Vous pourrez couvrir un périmètre large en matière de paiement et pourrez être amené.e à collaborer avec le cabinet Utsit, appartenant au groupe et expert des paiements B2B et des systèmes de trésorerie.

Car ce qui n'a pas de prix, c'est de pouvoir s'épanouir au sein de l'équipe. Et en faire profiter tout le monde.

#MatchingTheWorlds

Des exemples de missions?

https://www.oaklen.eu/success-story/accompagner-un-acteur-du-luxe-pour-deployer-son-projet-de-commerce-unifie-avec-adyen

Profil Recherché

Vous êtes diplômé·e (Bac +5) d’une école d’Ingénieur, de Commerce, d’une formation universitaire ou justifiez d’une première expérience dans notre secteur d’activité.

Vous êtes doté·e d’un esprit d'analyse et de synthèse, êtes rigoureux·se, méthodique et avez également le sens de l'initiative. De plus, des qualités relationnelles et communicationnelles sont nécessaires pour intégrer nos équipes et celles de nos interlocuteurs.

Une expérience passée dans les paiements est un plus. Mais ce que nous recherchons avant tout, c'est un esprit curieux, audacieux et motivé, avec l'envie d'échanger et de partager.

Si vous pensez que ça peut matcher, cliquez pour venir nous rencontrer.

companyDescription:
BNP Paribas is a leading bank in Europe with an international reach. It has a presence in 64 countries, with more than 178,000 employees, including more than 144,000 in Europe. 
BNP Paribas holds leading positions in its three major operating divisions:

⚆ Commercial, Personal Banking & Services for all the Group’s retail banking networks and several specialised businesses, including BNP Paribas Personal Finance and Arval;
⚆ Investment & Protection Services for savings, investment and protection solutions; 
⚆ Corporate & Institutional Banking, which is focused on corporate and institutional clients.

The Group helps all its clients (individuals, community associations, entrepreneurs, SMEs, corporates and institutional clients) to realise their projects through solutions spanning financing, investment, savings and protection insurance. 
In Europe, the Group has four domestic markets (Belgium, France, Italy and Luxembourg) and BNP Paribas Personal Finance is the European leader in consumer lending.
BNP Paribas is rolling out its integrated retail-banking model in Mediterranean countries, in Turkey, in Eastern Europe and a large network in the western part of the United States. In its Corporate & Institutional Banking and Investment & Protection Services activities, BNP Paribas also enjoys top positions in Europe, a strong presence in the Americas as well as a solid and fast-growing business in Asia-Pacific.

To learn more about our social media terms and Data Protection Notice: https://group.bnpparibas/en/data-protection
descriptionHtml:
Votre rôle au quotidien

Vous intégrez la Conformité de BNP Paribas CIB au sein d’une équipe en charge des grandes Institutions Financières, partenaire du Coverage Financial Institutions FIC, du métier Securities Services, de la Business Line Correspondant Banking CBK ou de la fonction Group Business Services GBS.

La mission de Compliance-CIB-Financial Institutions est d'accompagner le Coverage FIC, GM - les métiers Securities Services et CBK - la fonction GBS dans le développement d'une culture durable sur les sujets Conformité.

En tant que partenaire et conseiller indépendant, l'équipe contribue à donner l'assurance raisonnable que le dispositif conformité des activités de ces Business Units répond aux normes réglementaires externes et aux politiques internes du Groupe, avec une première ligne métier responsable en premier lieu de ses risques.

L'équipe se compose de 7 personnes traitant des sujets de prise de décision sur des dossiers Clients ou Transactions.

Vos Missions Au Quotidien

  • Participation quotidienne à l'activité BAU clients de l'équipe principalement orienté onboarding/recertification de clients FIC/GM/2S/CBK ou GBS sensibles (quelques transactions à revoir).

Ce travail comprend des actions de contrôle qualité, d'analyse et de préparation de recommandation dans une dimension multi-sites.

  • Préparation active des différentes réunions d'équipe qui permettent de suivre notre activité et d'améliorer la convergence de nos décisions (dashboard, cercle de qualité) et contribution aux réunions de gouvernance de l’équipe.
  • Contribution aux différentes taches BAU moins fréquentes, essentiellement liées au dispositif de contrôle permanent des Business Lines suivies par l'équipe. Contribution aux revues de portefeuille.
  • Contribution aux projets auxquels l'équipe peut participer et qui souvent sont liés à la consultation ou revue pour simplification des procédures compliance clefs impactant nos Business Lines; souvent autour des sujets KYC.

Le poste situé à Paris 75019 dans l’immeuble Millénaire 1 est ouvert pour un CDD de 6 mois à compter de novembre 2025.

Les avantages à nous rejoindre

Un Package Rémunération Et Des Avantages

  • un fixe annuel brut
  • un plan épargne entreprise/retraite, intéressement et participation, couverture santé et prévoyance, activités sociales et culturelles via le comité d’entreprise, …
  • de la flexibilité avec un rythme de de travail hybride: jusqu’à 2,5 jours de télétravail à définir avec votre manager.

Rejoignez un Groupe engagé et prenez part à notre grand projet de transformation vers la construction d’un monde plus durable. Découvrez nos engagements pour notre clientèle et la société.

Engagez-vous à nos côtés sur votre temps professionnel, à travers notre programme OneMillionHoursToHelp.

Avez-vous le profil ?

Vous êtes diplômé(e) Bac+5 (écoles de management, Ingénieur ou 3ème cycle universitaire).

Vous avez une expérience de 3 ans minimum dans la Fonction Conformité d’une banque d’investissement, et plus particulièrement sur les sujets de KYC.

Vous avez également de bonnes connaissances des problématiques sanctions et AML.

Doté d’un bon relationnel, vous êtes organisé et autonome.

Vous êtes reconnus pour votre capacité d’analyse, votre curiosité et votre capacité à être chercher des informations et émettre des positions éclairées.

Enfin, vous êtes capable de communiquer efficacement à l’écrit comme à l’oral en français et en anglais, et avez déjà évoluer dans un environnement de travail international.

Les étapes du recrutement

Si votre CV est retenu par notre équipe de recrutement, vous serez amené à passer un à trois entretiens au maximum avec des RH (Staffing Business Partner) et/ou manager opérationnel.

Si vous êtes dans une situation de handicap et souhaitez un échange facilité, vous pouvez envoyer votre CV et lettre de motivation à missionhandicap@bnpparibas.com.

Dans un monde qui change, la diversité, l’équité et l’inclusion sont des valeurs clés pour le bien-être et la performance des équipes. Chez BNP Paribas, nous souhaitons accueillir et retenir tous les talents sans distinction : c’est ainsi que nous construirons, ensemble, la finance de demain, innovante, responsable et durable

Enfin, nous attachons une importance particulière à ce que nos futurs collaborateurs et collaboratrices agissent au quotidien avec responsabilité éthique et professionnelle. À tout moment pendant le processus de recrutement, les informations figurant sur votre CV, vos données d’identification et vos antécédents pourront être vérifiés.

Bank of America is one of the world's largest financial institutions, serving individuals, small- and middle-market businesses and large corporations with a full range of banking, investing, asset management and other financial and risk management products and services. The company serves approximately 56 million U.S. consumer and small business relationships. It is among the world's leading wealth management companies and is a global leader in corporate and investment banking and trading.

This LinkedIn company page is moderated. For more information, please visit: https://bit.ly/32FDdQr.

descriptionHtml:
Job Description:

Job Title: Markets Compliance Specialist

Corporate Title: AVP/VP

Location: Paris

Company Overview:

At Bank of America, we are guided by a common purpose to help make financial lives better through the power of every connection. Responsible Growth is how we run our company and how we deliver for our clients, teammates, communities and shareholders every day.

One of the keys to driving Responsible Growth is being a great place to work for our teammates around the world. We’re devoted to being a diverse and inclusive workplace for everyone. We hire individuals with a broad range of backgrounds and experiences and invest heavily in our teammates and their families by offering competitive benefits to support their physical, emotional, and financial well-being.

Bank of America believes both in the importance of working together and offering flexibility to our employees. We use a multi-faceted approach for flexibility, depending on the various roles in our organization.

Working at Bank of America will give you a great career with opportunities to learn, grow and make an impact, along with the power to make a difference. Join us!

Location Overview:

Based in the 8th arrondissement and within the Parisian Golden Triangle, our beautiful Art-Deco era office benefits from luxury modern workspaces, an abundance of natural light and is easily accessible by public transport.

The Team:

The Paris-based Compliance and Operational Risk team engage in activities to provide independent compliance and operational risk oversight of Front-Line Unit/Control Function (CF) performance and any related third party/vendor relationships in alignment with the Global Compliance – Enterprise Policy.

Role Description:

The Markets Compliance Specialist is responsible for executing second line of defense Compliance and Operational risk oversight for the Equities front-office team.

Responsibilities:

  • Assist in assessing risks, associated controls and their effectiveness, while driving compliance with applicable laws, rules, and regulations, adherence to policies, and developing reporting and documentation.
  • Engage in activities to provide support to the Compliance & Operational Risk team to provide compliance and operational risk oversight of Front-Line Unit or Control Function performance.
  • Assist in identifying and escalating problems or issues that arise and drive actions to address the root causes that result in compliance risk issues and/or operational risk losses.
  • Assist in the development of risk management reporting for respective area(s) of coverage as input into management routines.
  • Conduct independent reviews and monitoring of the Front-Line Unit.
  • Assist in responding to trading venue and regulatory inquiries, and other audits and/or examinations.
  • Assist with investigation of escalated trade surveillance alerts and continual enhancement of the trade surveillance program elements.
  • Apply knowledge of the regulatory environment and applying regulatory changes to area(s) of coverage.

What we are looking for:

  • A versatile, confident individual with relevant work experience in a front-office/Markets Compliance team.
  • Ability to engage and communicate confidently and professionally with Trading and Sales teams, and with other internal stakeholders.
  • Knowledge of relevant EU and US regulations, including but not limited to MiFID II, MAR, Dodd Frank, Volcker.
  • Ability to interpret relevant laws, rules and regulations.
  • Excellent communication skills and strong English language skills are essential.

Skills that will help:

  • Ability to work in a high-energy and demanding environment, taking care of multiple deliverables.

Benefits of working at Bank of America:

France

  • Competitive retirement plan in addition to State plans
  • Mandatory Medical Plan provided by the bank as a top-up to Social Security health benefits for you and your family.
  • Employees and family members can receive free health advice 24/7 through a range of English and French speaking medical providers
  • Life and disability insurance
  • Time Savings Account (‘CET’) to save some of your leave days, cash them out or use them later
  • Reimbursement of 50% of your commuter pass
  • Nursery scheme for children under age 3
  • Works council benefits
  • Access to an Employee Assistance Programme for confidential support and help for everyday matters
  • Opportunity to give back to your community, develop new skills and work with new groups of people by volunteering with local charities
  • Opportunity to receive free entry to arts exhibitions sponsored by Bank of America in Paris

Bank of America:

Good conduct and sound judgment is crucial to our long term success. It’s important that all employees in the organisation understand the expected standards of conduct and how we manage conduct risk. Individual accountability and an ownership mind-set are the cornerstones of our Code of Conduct and are at the heart of managing risk well.

We are an equal opportunities employer and ensure that no applicant is subject to less favourable treatment on the grounds of sex, gender identity or gender reassignment, marital or civil partner status, race, religion or belief, colour, nationality, ethnic or national origins, age, sexual orientation, pregnancy or maternity, socio-economic background, responsibility for dependants or physical or mental disability. The Bank selects candidates for interview based on their skills, qualifications and experience.

We strive to ensure that our recruitment processes are accessible for all candidates and encourage any candidates to tell us about any adjustment requirements.

companyDescription: Arkéa Asset Management, société de gestion d’actifs du groupe Crédit Mutuel Arkéa, se distingue comme un acteur majeur et engagé de la gestion d’actifs, recherchant performance financière et extra-financière. Grâce à son adossement à un groupe bancaire solide et à sa taille (200 collaborateurs et 55 milliards d’euros d’encours sous gestion), elle allie agilité et engagement pour proposer une offre diversifiée (gestion cotée, dette privée et solutions d’investissement) adaptée aux besoins de ses clients particuliers et institutionnels.
companySlogan: Performance engagée au service de l'humain et de son environnement
descriptionHtml:
Description du poste

Métier

METIERS - Audit / Risques / Contrôle / Sécurité

Intitulé du poste

Chargé.e de Conformité et Contrôle Interne H/F

Contrat

CDI

Votre mission

Au sein de l’équipe Conformité et Contrôle Interne (7 personnes, dont 5 à Brest et 2 à Paris), vous travaillerez sur des thématiques de contrôle interne, conformité et vos principales missions seront de :

  • Mettre en œuvre le plan de contrôle annuel ;
  • Participer à l’élaboration et la revue des procédures internes ;
  • Suivre les incidents opérationnels et les plans d’actions correctifs ;
  • Participer au dispositif de prévention des abus de marché
  • Cartographier les risques et élaborer les tableaux de bord et d’indicateurs de suivi ;
  • Prévenir les risques de non-conformité en matière de sécurité financière liés au blanchiment de capitaux et au financement du terrorisme (LCB/FT) et au non-respect des mesures de sanctions & embargos
  • Réaliser des déclarations de soupçon en tant que correspondant/déclarant TRACFIN,
  • Préparer les reportings réglementaires (LCB-FT, FRA-RAC…) ;
  • Contribuer à la formation et à la sensibilisation des équipes internes ;
  • Vous participez aux contrôles AMF et audits internes ou externes ;

Localisation : Paris avec déplacement occasionnel à Brest

Et si c'était vous ?

Diplômé(e) d'un Bac+5 en Ecole de Commerce ou Université avec une spécialité en Droit des affaires (idéalement Master 2 Droit Bancaire et Financier / Conformité), vous avez une expérience de cinq ans en conformité et/ou en droit des marchés acquise au sein d'une société de gestion, d’un PSI ou d’un établissement financier.

Vous recherchez une polyvalence dans les missions, votre curiosité, votre rigueur, votre capacité à organiser et à prioriser les tâches, seront des atouts pour intégrer l'équipe.

La maîtrise de l'anglais (niveau courant) est indispensable.

Qui sommes-nous ?

Cette opportunité s'inscrit au sein de la société Arkea Asset Management, filiale du Groupe Crédit Mutuel Arkéa qui figure aujourd'hui parmi les principaux acteurs bancaires sur le marché français et européen.

Arkéa Asset Management, fruit du regroupement de Federal Finance Gestion et de Schelcher Prince Gestion, est une société de gestion d’actifs du Groupe Crédit Mutuel Arkéa, qui se distingue comme un acteur majeur et responsable de la gestion d’actifs, recherchant performances financière et extra-financière. Grâce à son adossement à un groupe bancaire solide et à sa taille humaine, Arkéa Asset Management allie agilité et engagement pour proposer une offre diversifiée en gestion cotée, dette privée et solutions d’investissement adaptées aux besoins de ses clients particuliers et institutionnels.

Lieu

Paris

Critères candidat

Niveau d'études min. requis

BAC+5 OU PLUS

Langues

ANGLAIS (COURANT)

companyDescription:
Square Facts, a division of iCOVER, oﬀers compliance and risk solutions by combining human intelligence with advanced technology. Our third-party screening, monitoring, and due diligence services provide valuable insights for overall compliance needs, from entry-level screening to investigative reports on individuals and organizations.
By tapping into over 800,000 curated sources, we deliver thorough anti-money laundering, anti-bribery and corruption, and counter-financing terrorism reports with speed and precision at a competitive price.

At Square Facts, we aim to simplify the complex process of complying with a constantly evolving regulatory landscape for our clients. Our approach involves utilizing the latest technology and data science, while also incorporating the expertise of our experienced analysts to provide clear ready-touse reports.
companySlogan: Your global expert in third-party risk mitigation
descriptionHtml:
La société Square Facts (Groupe iCOVER) est un leader européen de la gestion des risques Éthique et Compliance. Dans une optique de sécurisation des relations d’affaires et de leur mise en conformité avec les obligations de vigilance, notre société accompagne des clients internationaux issus de divers secteurs d’activités.


Notre entreprise combine une expertise unique en intelligence humaine et économique avec des technologies de pointe et une plateforme API. Nos services de vérification et de due diligence standards et approfondies apportent à nos clients des données exploitables pour répondre à leurs besoins globaux en matière de conformité, du vérification des tiers en matière d'AML/CFT.


Pour accompagner sa croissance, Square Facts recherche un(e) :


Analyste Senior (H-F)


Basé(e) à Paris et rattaché(e) au Responsable des Opérations Risques & Conformité, l'Analyste Senior(e) aura pour principales missions l’élaboration de Due Diligence, l’analyse de risques, et les recherches en sources ouvertes ou en intelligence humaine.


Responsabilités


  • Rechercher, traiter et analyser des informations accessibles au public, notamment des articles de presse, registres d'entreprises, bases de données privées, archives web, etc.
  • Collecter, analyser et examiner des informations publiques pour la réalisation de vérifications d’antécédents dans différentes juridictions, en vue d'établir de nouveaux process.
  • Rédiger des rapports de due diligence, des rapports d'intégrité et d'autres produits de conformité selon des standards éditoriaux exigeants.
  • Identifier et contacter des sources pour mener des entretiens téléphoniques avec des experts sectoriels et des journalistes ou tout autres sources pertinentes
  • Identifier de nouvelles sources d'information (bases de données, registres, administrations locales, correspondants…) ; développer des sources trans-sectorielles via la recherche et le réseautage.
  • Développer un portefeuille de sources humaines et de prestataires externes pour soutenir l'équipe dans son travail d'intelligence humaine
  • Travailler individuellement et/ou en équipe avec les analystes ou des partenaires externes sur des missions assignées, en utilisant des compétences d'analyse et de recherche.
  • A terme, participer à la revue de certains rapports produits par les analystes.
  • Assurer proactivement et en autonomie le suivi et le support d’un portefeuille client attribué.
  • Gérer des projets de mise à jour ou de créations de process et méthodologie interne de production.
  • S’impliquer dans le processus mise à jour ou la création de produits


Profil recherché


  • Diplôme universitaire en Droit, Journalisme, Économie, Sciences Politiques ou équivalent.
  • Expérience préalable (4 à 6 ans) en due diligence, conseil en conformité ou investigations.
  • Maîtrise avérée des techniques de recherche en sources ouvertes.
  • Expérience confirmée en intelligence humaine : identification de sources qualifiées, conduite d’entretiens, analyse et intégration des transcriptions dans les rapports.
  • Expérience débutante ou appétence pour la gestion de portefeuille client sur les expertises de notre secteur.
  • Excellente maîtrise de l'anglais et du français écrit et parlé.
  • Idéalement bonne maîtrise d’une troisième langue. Intérêt particulier pour : chinois, arabe ou espagnol.
  • Solides compétences analytiques et de recherche.
  • Très bonnes compétences en communication écrite et orale.
  • Excellente gestion du temps.
  • Attitude tournée vers la résolution de problèmes et la qualité.
  • Rigueur et sens du détail.


Ce que nous attendons de vous


  • Être un(e) Team Player.
  • Être proactif(ve) dans l'identification des problèmes et de leurs solutions.
  • Avoir le goût d'apprendre de nouvelles choses.
  • Être à l'aise dans un environnement international.
  • Être prêt(e) à partager des moments conviviaux avec l'équipe.
  • Faire preuve d'excellentes compétences en résolution de problèmes, en pensée critique et en communication.


Pourquoi nous choisir?


  • Un poste stimulant dans une entreprise internationale à taille humaine.
  • Un environnement de travail dynamique et une équipe enthousiaste.
  • Un package salarial attractif et un bon équilibre vie professionnelle / vie personnelle.
  • Politique de télétravail.
  • Bureaux idéalement situés au cœur de Paris (Bourse)
  • Tickets restaurant, assurance santé, remboursement du transport en commun, contribution annuelle aux activités sportives.


Pour en savoir plus sur nous : https://square-facts.com/


Intéressé(e) ?


Square Facts est un employeur garantissant l'égalité des chances, engagé à créer un environnement de travail inclusif où tous nos collaborateurs sont encouragés à atteindre leur plein potentiel, et où les différences individuelles sont valorisées et respectées. Toutes les candidatures qualifiées sont examinées sans considération de race, couleur, religion, sexe, orientation sexuelle, identité de genre, âge, origine nationale, statut de handicap ou toute autre caractéristique protégée par la législation locale.

En soumettant votre candidature pour ce poste, vous acceptez qu'en cas de sélection, Square Facts soit autorisée à effectuer une vérification appropriée des antécédents, notamment, mais sans s'y limiter, une vérification de votre parcours professionnel et académique. La collecte et le traitement licite de vos données personnelles sont nécessaires aux fins des intérêts légitimes poursuivis par Square Facts et seront effectués dans le strict respect de la législation applicable en matière de protection des données personnelles.

companyDescription:
LSEG (London Stock Exchange Group) is a diversified international markets infrastructure business —earning our clients’ trust for over 300 years. That legacy of customer-focused excellence ensures that you can rely on our expertise in capital formation, intellectual property and risk and balance sheet management. 

As global leaders in financial indexing, benchmarking and analytic services, we offer unrivalled access to international capital markets. Our high-performance technology solutions enable companies worldwide to access funds for growth and development. And with our Data & Analytics, Capital Markets and Post Trade divisions, we provide a comprehensive, integrated suite of trusted financial market infrastructure services that help our customers pursue—and achieve—their ambitions. 

You can count on our open access model for unparalleled partnership, flexibility, stability, and support across all of our businesses. That’s how we make a difference— ensuring people can meet their potential—worldwide.
descriptionHtml:
LSEG (London Stock Exchange Group) is more than a diversified global financial markets infrastructure and data business. We are dedicated, open-access partners with a commitment to excellence in delivering the services our customers expect from us. With extensive experience, deep knowledge and worldwide presence across financial markets, we enable businesses and economies to fund innovation, handle risk and create jobs. It’s how we support the financial stability and growth of communities and economies globally for more than 300 years.

Through a comprehensive suite of trusted financial market infrastructure services – and our open-access model – we provide the flexibility, stability and trust that enable our customers to pursue their ambitions with confidence and visibility.

LSEG is headquartered in the United Kingdom, with significant operations in 70 countries across EMEA, North America, Latin America and Asia Pacific. We employ 25,000 people globally, more than half located in Asia Pacific. LSEG’s ticker symbol is LSEG.

Within the Markets division of LSEG, LCH is a group of leading multi-asset clearing houses that provides risk management capabilities across a range of asset classes. As demand for robust clearing services continues to grow, LCH is committed to maintaining the highest standards of risk management across all our services.

LCH operates an open access model, offering a choice of execution venues, delivering outstanding choice and efficiencies to the marketplace.

LCH operates clearing houses around the world, with clearing houses incorporated in the UK, France and the United States with additional offices in the Asia Pacific region. It offers clearing services across asset classes including OTC and listed rates; CDS and FX; fixed income; commodities; cash equities and equity derivatives.

Overall functional objective

Operational Resilience is transversal to all departments of the organization. There is therefore an increased need for central coordination. The proposed role would be the first line of defence for third party risk management (TPRM).

LCH SA must align with many regulations and have the highest standards in risk management in general and in operational resilience in particular. As part of its operational resilience framework, LCH SA has implemented a TPRM framework.

This framework is already live and align with the one Group level but needs to evolve to follow new regulation requirements.

The role will ensure the regime of activities required as per the policy and standards is undertaken by Risk Owners (i.e. Relationship Owners).

The role is the central point of contact for all TPRM topics.

This role is part of the COO office team and reports to the Head of First Line Resilience.

Operational responsibilities:

  • Coordinate with Relationship Owners the onboarding of new third parties and due diligences activities
  • Oversee TPRM controls
  • Maintain the TPRM register up to date and ensure the accuracy of data
  • Ensure the appropriate reporting of TPRM activities
  • Ensure a consolidated view of TPRM (consolidation of IT Outsourcers with IT TPRM team)
  • Collaborate with the Group, Post Trade/Markets Division and SLR to align LCH SA TPRM framework with the Group’s
  • Ensure the Relationship owners respect the framework, work with them on remediation plans and monitor their progress
  • Organize and chair the TPRM Working Group
  • Be the central point of contact for TPRM matters with Group, SLR, authorities
  • Be a proposition force and inspire changes for all TPRM-related topics (new metrics to follow, organization of folders and documents, help other departments with TPRM requirements…)
  • Collaborate with the Operational Resilience Programme to ensure the correct representation of third party risk in the broader framework

Develop and manage main partnerships with:

  • LSEG TPRM central teams
  • Relationship owners
  • Second Line Risk with respect to frameworks and risk management
  • COOs of Business Lines to ensure good business awareness
  • Other Risk Domains leads (for sharing expertise, ensure consistency between frameworks and build synergies)
  • IT Third Party Manager
  • Liaise with regulators when required.

Experience Required

  • At least 10 years of relevant experience in the financial industry, with technology and risk management dimensions
  • Excellent verbal and written communication skills in English
  • Excellent organization and planning skills
  • High level of rigour
  • Capacity to synthetize and adapt to audience
  • Ability to interact with multiple teams and to foster a positive influence
  • Knowledge of financial markets
  • Knowledge of third party risk management
  • Knowledge of regulatory environment

LSEG is a leading global financial markets infrastructure and data provider. Our purpose is driving financial stability, empowering economies and enabling customers to create sustainable growth.

Our purpose is the foundation on which our culture is built. Our values of Integrity, Partnership, Excellence and Change underpin our purpose and set the standard for everything we do, every day. They go to the heart of who we are and guide our decision making and everyday actions.

Working with us means that you will be part of a dynamic organisation of 25,000 people across 65 countries. However, we will value your individuality and enable you to bring your true self to work so you can help enrich our diverse workforce. You will be part of a collaborative and creative culture where we encourage new ideas and are committed to sustainability across our global business. You will experience the critical role we have in helping to re-engineer the financial ecosystem to support and drive sustainable economic growth. Together, we are aiming to achieve this growth by accelerating the just transition to net zero, enabling growth of the green economy and creating inclusive economic opportunity.

LSEG offers a range of tailored benefits and support, including healthcare, retirement planning, paid volunteering days and wellbeing initiatives.

We are proud to be an equal opportunities employer. This means that we do not discriminate on the basis of anyone’s race, religion, colour, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, marital status, veteran status, pregnancy or disability, or any other basis protected under applicable law. Conforming with applicable law, we can reasonably accommodate applicants' and employees' religious practices and beliefs, as well as mental health or physical disability needs.

Please take a moment to read this privacy notice carefully, as it describes what personal information London Stock Exchange Group (LSEG) (we) may hold about you, what it’s used for, and how it’s obtained, your rights and how to contact us as a data subject.

If you are submitting as a Recruitment Agency Partner, it is essential and your responsibility to ensure that candidates applying to LSEG are aware of this privacy notice.

companyDescription:
People deserve more from their money. More visibility, more control, and more freedom. Since 2015, Revolut has been on a mission to deliver just that. Our powerhouse of products help our 50+ million customers get more from their money every day.

As we continue our lightning-fast growth,‌ 2 things are essential to our success: our people and our culture. In recognition of our outstanding employee experience, we've been certified as a Great Place to Work™.

So far, we have 10,000+ people working around the world, from our offices and remotely, to help us achieve our mission. And we're looking for more brilliant people. People who love building great products, redefining success, and turning the complexity of a chaotic world into the simplicity of a beautiful solution.
companySlogan: Change the way you money. For support related queries, please send a message through our LinkedIn Revolut Support page.
descriptionHtml:
About Revolut

People deserve more from their money. More visibility, more control, and more freedom. Since 2015, Revolut has been on a mission to deliver just that. Our powerhouse of products — including spending, saving, investing, exchanging, travelling, and more — help our 60+ million customers get more from their money every day.

As we continue our lightning-fast growth,‌ 2 things are essential to our success: our people and our culture. In recognition of our outstanding employee experience, we've been certified as a Great Place to Work™. So far, we have 10,000+ people working around the world, from our offices and remotely, to help us achieve our mission. And we're looking for more brilliant people. People who love building great products, redefining success, and turning the complexity of a chaotic world into the simplicity of a beautiful solution.

About The Role

Our Compliance team blends regulatory expertise with data-driven thinking to make sure our products meet legal and policy requirements and deliver real value to customers. In a fast-moving, digital environment, they stay one step ahead by finding smart, scalable ways to manage conduct risk.

We’re looking for a Compliance Assurance Manager who’ll help us improve all aspects of finance and risk management.

Up for the challenge? Let's get in touch.

What You'll Be Doing

  • Assisting with the design and implementation of the compliance testing and assurance programme, including the risk-based compliance assessment, control testing, and assurance plan
  • Completing assurance reviews and testing activities, leveraging skills in advanced data analytics
  • Establishing automated queries and dashboards to drive more frequent testing and informative insights
  • Contributing to compliance processes by identifying opportunities for improved adherence to regulatory requirements and company standards
  • Executing project plans to address remediation efforts and testing review findings, including process updates, documentation, and/or training
  • Driving execution of multiple concurrent reviews, which may be local or multi-jurisdictional, including subsequent management reporting related to review outcomes and findings
  • Challenging colleagues to build efficient, world-class risk management controls and processes
  • Using data analytics to identify actionable insights, which result in material enhancements to the control environment

What You'll Need

  • 3+ years of experience working within a fintech, traditional financial institution, or regulatory body
  • Relevant experience in assurance or internal/external audit, focusing on risk and compliance domains
  • Familiarity with assurance/audit reviews
  • Experience in controls, risk, and policy and procedure testing
  • Excellent stakeholder engagement skills to hold the 1LoD accountable for regulatory compliance matters
  • Experience interrogating large data sets to identify actionable insights
  • To be results-driven, pragmatic, and have a detailed-oriented approach
  • An interest in compliance and willingness to deepen your knowledge
  • Experience creating or utilising test scripts/procedures
  • Relevant financial crime and compliance qualifications, such as ACAMS and ICA
  • Experience working on financial crime risks
  • Fluency in English

Nice to have

  • Experience in payment screening, regulatory reporting, SAR filing, and external enquiries
  • Knowledge of sanction and PEP regulations
  • Expertise in rule-based or model-based AML transaction monitoring, threshold tuning, and below-the-line testing
  • Experience identifying and preventing onboarding risks for retail (KYC) and business (KYB) customers
  • Experience in risk assessments or review of FCA regulations and EU AML directives
  • Experience designing, implementing, or testing customer risk scoring and segmentation
  • Data analytics skills, including SQL and/or Python

Building a global financial super app isn’t enough. Our Revoluters are a priority, and that’s why in 2021 we launched our inaugural D&I Framework, designed to help us thrive and grow everyday. We're not just doing this because it's the right thing to do. We’re doing it because we know that seeking out diverse talent and creating an inclusive workplace is the way to create exceptional, innovative products and services for our customers. That’s why we encourage applications from people with diverse backgrounds and experiences to join this multicultural, hard-working team.

Important notice for candidates:

Job scams are on the rise. Please keep these guidelines in mind when applying for any open roles.

  • Only apply through official Revolut channels. We don’t use any third-party services or platforms for our recruitment.
  • Always double-check the emails you receive. Make sure all communications are being done through official Revolut emails, with an @revolut.com domain.

We won't ask for payment or personal financial information during the hiring process. If anyone does ask you for this, it’s a scam. Report it immediately.

By submitting this application, I confirm that all the information given by me in this application for employment and any additional documents attached hereto are true to the best of my knowledge and that I have not wilfully suppressed any material fact. I confirm I have disclosed if applicable any previous employment with Revolut. I accept that if any of the information given by me in this application is in any way false or incorrect, my application may be rejected, any offer of employment may be withdrawn or my employment with Revolut may be terminated summarily or I may be dismissed. By submitting this application, I agree that my personal data will be processed in accordance with Revolut's <a class="postings-link" href="https://www.revolut.com/legal/data-privacy-for-candidates">Candidate Privacy Notice

companyDescription:
L'Union des Entreprises et des Salariés pour le Logement (UESL) est une société anonyme à capital variable à Conseil de Surveillance et Directoire qui a pour principal objet de représenter les intérêts communs de ses associés et de mettre en œuvre les politiques nationales d’emploi des fonds d’Action Logement.


Ses associés sont, à titre obligatoire, chaque organisme collecteur (CIL) et chaque Chambre de Commerce et d’Industrie (CCI) agréés aux fins de collecter et d’utiliser les fonds de la PEEC, à la demande, toute organisation interprofessionnelle et représentative au plan national de salariés ou d’entreprises assujetties au versement de la participation des employeurs à l’effort de construction. 	
Trois commissaires du gouvernement représentent l'État au Conseil de Surveillance.
Pour la mise en œuvre de ces missions, elle assure l’animation et la coordination du réseau des CIL et dispose de fonds d'intervention.
companySlogan: Action Logement, facilite l'accès au logement des salariés pour favoriser l'emploi, partout en France. #VOULOIRETLEFAIRE
descriptionHtml:
Le groupe Action logement a été créé en 2017 pour être le collecteur unique de la PEEC (Participation des employeurs à l'effort de construction) et ainsi financer le logement des salariés à revenus modestes. Action Logement finance aussi la construction du logement social, participe au financement des politiques publiques et met à disposition des aides et services aux particuliers. Ces aides concernent autant la location, que l'achat et le financement des projets de travaux.

Vouloir améliorer le logement... ET LE FAIRE !

Rejoignez un Groupe qui agit concrètement pour faciliter le logement des salariés et ainsi améliorer le quotidien de millions de Français.

Le groupe Action Logement, employeur de référence dans son secteur, couvre l'ensemble des métiers de service et de l'immobilier. À ce jour, le Groupe compte plus de 100 métiers répartis dans quatre grands domaines d'expertises : l'aide et l'accompagnement des bénéficiaires, la gestion du parc immobilier, la conception et la construction de logements durables, l'amélioration et l'innovation au service de toutes les activités du Groupe.

Dans ce contexte, nous recherchons un Analyste conformité LCB-FT H/F pour notre entité Action Logement Services en cdd d'une durée de 6 mois avec une possibilité de prolongation de la durée selon les besoins de l'entreprise à l'issue de cette période.  

A ce titre, vos principales missions :

Le traitement des alertes PPE-GDA-Antécédents médiatiques
Le traitement des alertes opérations atypiques
La réalisation contrôles conformité
Les réponses aux sollicitations métiers
La réalisation de formations LCB-FT à destination des métiers
La participation aux ateliers du projet Entrée en relation


De formation supérieure du type Bac+4/5 en Banque/Finance, vous bénéficiez d'au moins trois ans d'expérience professionnelle en LCB-FT au sein d'un établissement bancaire ou de logement.

Afin de réussir dans la réalisation de ces missions, vous avez développé une connaissance approfondie de la règlementation bancaire.

Votre engagement et votre proactivité pour toutes démarches d'élaboration, d'amélioration des processus seront des atouts pour réussir vos missions.

Autonomie et rigueur seront des pré requis indispensables.

Vous possédez de réelles capacités d'adaptation à un environnement évolutif, ainsi que de très bonnes qualités relationnelles et rédactionnelles.

Le poste est à pourvoir en CDD sur Paris (75013).

Vous vous reconnaissez dans ce descriptif, alors n'hésitez plus et venez nous rencontrer !

Action Logement, société socialement responsable, s'engage au quotidien pour l'emploi des personnes en situation de handicap
